project.name: metaphysics_core
project.description: Shared core library for Xuan Metaphysics projects: domain enums, json_serializable models and DTOs, and metaphysics calculators (four-pillars/BaZi, da_yun, liu_yun, tai_yuan, datetime). Declared Pure Dart in pubspec but a few files still depend on Flutter; a migration-consolidation target (code moved in from xuan-common).

nodes.name: enums.dart
nodes.summary: Central enums barrel for the metaphysics_core package, re-exporting 30 enum files (DiZhi, TianGan, JiaZi, JieQi, etc.) so consumers can import all domain enums from a single entry point.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure re-export barrel; pairs with lib/models/enums.dart which separately re-exports layout_template_enums.dart, forming a dual enums-barrel arrangement.

nodes.name: enums.dart
nodes.summary: Secondary enums shim that re-exports layout_template_enums.dart for backward compatibility and additionally defines the ColorPreviewMode enum; complements the main lib/enums.dart barrel.
nodes.languageNotes: Thin re-export shim plus one locally-defined enum (ColorPreviewMode).

nodes.name: datetime_strategy_enums.dart
nodes.summary: Defines datetime/solar-term calculation strategy enums (ZiShiStrategy, JieQiType, JieQiStrategy, PhenologyStrategy, JieQiEntryPrecision) controlling how Zi-hour boundaries and solar-term subdivisions are computed.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON-serializable enums via @JsonEnum/@JsonValue from json_annotation; pure Dart, no Flutter contamination.

nodes.name: enum_28_constellations.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the Enum28Constellations enhanced enum representing the 28 lunar mansions, each carrying its star name, associated seven-zheng star (EnumStars), and zodiac animal.
nodes.languageNotes: Dart enhanced enum with fields plus a static lookup (fromStarName); pure Dart with json_annotation only.

nodes.name: enum_di_zhi.dart
nodes.summary: Core Earthly Branches (地支) module defining DiZhi plus its relationship enums (DiZhiHe, DiZhiXing, DiZhiChong, DiZhiPo, DiZhiHai, DiZhiSanHui, DiZhiSanHe, DiZhiFourZheng, DiZhiRelationship) with rich five-element, yin-yang, hidden-stem and combination logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest enum module in the batch (10 enhanced enums, ~785 lines); heavy static factory + switch logic. Pure Dart with json_annotation only.

nodes.name: enum_five_xing_relationship.dart
nodes.summary: Defines FiveXingRelationship (生克泄耗同) with a static relationship lookup matrix, and FiveEnergyStatus (旺相休囚死) computing five-element strength by branch and by season.
nodes.languageNotes: Enhanced enums with a static const nested Map relationship matrix and large season/branch switch logic. Pure Dart.

nodes.name: enum_hou_tian_gua.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the eight-trigram enums XianTianGua (pre-heaven), Enum8Gua (with binary representations and conversions), and HouTianGua (post-heaven, with palace numbers, five-element, branches, and opposition checks).
nodes.languageNotes: Three enhanced enums with binary-string conversions and palace-opposition logic. Pure Dart.

nodes.name: enum_jia_zi.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the 60 JiaZi sexagenary cycle enum (with stem/branch/number/NaYin), the NaYinFiveXing enum, and the JiaZiIndexMapper helper class providing index/NaYin mapping tables and xun/kongwang computations.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports external package:tuple (Tuple2) for kongwang pairs; contains a companion class and an extension (FiveXingGetter on NaYinFiveXing). Pure Dart, no Flutter.

nodes.name: enum_liu_qin.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the LiuQin (六亲) enum representing the six relations and provides factories deriving the relation from a stem/branch pair via five-element relationship or from a single character.
nodes.languageNotes: Enhanced enum with static derivation logic depending on FiveXingRelationship. Pure Dart.

nodes.name: enum_month_general.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the MonthGeneral (月将) enum mapping the twelve month-generals to their branch, name, and the solar-term segment (Tuple2 of TwentyFourJieQi) over which each governs, with month/branch/jieqi factories.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses external package:tuple for the jie-segment pair. Pure Dart, no json_annotation here.

nodes.name: enum_stars.dart
nodes.summary: Defines EnumStars (the seven luminaries plus four hidden 余奴 stars) with five-element/yin-yang/day-night attributes and zheng/yu mappings, plus the EnumStarsPriority comparable enum.
nodes.languageNotes: EnumStarsPriority implements Comparable<EnumStarsPriority>. Imports the package's own enums.dart barrel (circular-ish into the barrel). Pure Dart.

nodes.name: enum_three_yuan.dart
nodes.summary: Defines San Yuan Jiu Yun enums: YuanYunOrder (upper/middle/lower 元), YuanYunType (元/运 with year spans), and NineYun (the nine luck periods, each linked to a NineStarEnum).
nodes.languageNotes: JSON-serializable enums; also holds two private top-level Map constants. Pure Dart.

nodes.name: enum_tian_gan.dart
nodes.summary: Core Heavenly Stems (天干) module defining TianGan (with five-element, yin-yang, na-jia trigram, five-tiger, ten-gods derivation) and TianGanFiveCombine (天干五合) with combination factories.
nodes.languageNotes: Two enhanced enums with extensive switch logic; uses external package:tuple. Pure Dart, no Flutter.

nodes.name: enum_twelve_zhang_sheng.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the TwelveZhangSheng (十二长生) life-cycle stage enum with extensive static mapping tables from heavenly stems / five-elements to ordered earthly branches, plus stage-strength helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy static final Map tables keyed by TianGan and FiveXing. Pure Dart.

nodes.name: enum_twenty_four_jie_qi.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the TwentyFourJieQi (二十四节气) enum carrying order, season, month-token, solar angle and ecliptic gong for each solar term, with jie/qi listings, next/previous navigation and eight-jie / four-li classification.
nodes.languageNotes: Enhanced enum with cyclic next/previous getters and classification logic. Pure Dart.

nodes.name: layout_template_enums.dart
nodes.summary: Defines UI/layout template enums for chart rendering: PillarType (柱 types), RowType (row kinds), BorderType, and RowTextAlign; re-exported by both lib/enums.dart and lib/models/enums.dart.
nodes.languageNotes: Presentation-oriented enums but still pure Dart (json_annotation only); no Flutter widget imports despite UI semantics.

nodes.name: nine_star.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the NineStarEnum (九星) enhanced enum, each star carrying number, post-heaven trigram, color, dunjia/god/ephemeris/xuankong names and five-element, with rich lookup factories by number, element, color and name.
nodes.languageNotes: Named-parameter enhanced enum constructor; depends on HouTianGua. Pure Dart.

nodes.name: ColorPreviewMode
nodes.summary: Simple enum of color preview rendering modes (none, inwell, global, blackwhite, colorful, pure) defined only in the models shim.

nodes.name: ZiShiStrategy
nodes.summary: Zi-hour (子时) boundary strategy enum with seven serialized variants including legacy-compatible values controlling how 23:00-01:00 maps to day/hour pillars.

nodes.name: JieQiType
nodes.summary: Solar-term method enum distinguishing leveling (平气法) vs stabilizing (定气法) computation, each with a Chinese display name.

nodes.name: JieQiStrategy
nodes.summary: Enum selecting the granularity (day/hour/minute) at which solar-term boundaries divide a time point.

nodes.name: JieQiEntryPrecision
nodes.summary: Enum for the precision (shichen/hour/minute/second) of computed solar-term entry moments.

nodes.name: Enum28Constellations
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum of the 28 lunar mansions; each value binds a star name, its seven-zheng star (EnumStars) and zodiac animal, with a fullname getter and fromStarName lookup.

nodes.name: DiZhi
nodes.summary: The twelve Earthly Branches enhanced enum exposing five-element, yin-yang, hidden stems (cangGan), zodiac, ten-gods, six-chong/he/po/hai relations and many static set helpers (four horse/bath/tomb groups).

nodes.name: DiZhiRelationship
nodes.summary: Enum naming the kinds of branch relationships (刑冲合害破 plus half-combinations and 三会).

nodes.name: DiZhiHe
nodes.summary: Six-combination (六合) enum pairing branches with the resulting five-element, plus factories to resolve a combination from a single branch, branch pair, or string.

nodes.name: DiZhiXing
nodes.summary: Branch-punishment (刑) enum encoding the punishment groups (self/无礼/无恩/持势) with nicknames, content lists, and elaborate factories to detect punishments from sets or strings of branches.

nodes.name: DiZhiChong
nodes.summary: Six-clash (六冲) enum of opposing branch pairs with factories to resolve a clash from a single branch, pair, or string.

nodes.name: DiZhiPo
nodes.summary: Six-break (六破) enum of branch break-pairs with single/pair/string resolution factories.

nodes.name: DiZhiHai
nodes.summary: Six-harm (六害) enum of branch harm-pairs with single/pair/string resolution factories.

nodes.name: DiZhiSanHui
nodes.summary: Three-meeting (三会) directional branch-trio enum mapping each trio to its five-element, with factories by branch, string, trio, or element.

nodes.name: DiZhiSanHe
nodes.summary: Three-harmony (三合) branch-trio enum with element results, ordered/reversed sequences, horse-position lookup, and resolution factories by branch/string/trio/element.

nodes.name: DiZhiFourZheng
nodes.summary: Meng/Zhong/Ji (孟仲季 / four-cardinal) classification enum grouping branches into horse/bath/tomb sets with single-branch and other-branch helpers.

nodes.name: FiveXingRelationship
nodes.summary: Five-element relationship enum (生克泄耗同) backed by a static const nested lookup matrix, exposing checkRelationship and per-element relationship dictionaries.

nodes.name: FiveEnergyStatus
nodes.summary: Energy-state enum (旺相休囚死) computing five-element strength at a given branch and across the five seasons via large switch tables, with strong/weak predicates.

nodes.name: XianTianGua
nodes.summary: Pre-heaven eight-trigram enum binding each trigram to its order, name and direction.

nodes.name: Enum8Gua
nodes.summary: General eight-trigram enum carrying name, nickname and bottom-top binary string, with conversions to pre/post-heaven trigrams and binary-string factories.

nodes.name: HouTianGua
nodes.summary: Post-heaven eight-trigram (plus center) enum with palace order, five-element, one or two branches, binary strings, and palace-opposition (对冲) detection helpers.

nodes.name: NaYinFiveXing
nodes.summary: The 30 NaYin (纳音) five-element names enum with a value lookup; its fiveXing getter is provided via a separate extension.

nodes.name: JiaZiIndexMapper
nodes.summary: Static helper class holding the stem-branch to JiaZi map, the ordered 60-JiaZi list, and the NaYin-to-five-element mapping table used throughout JiaZi computations.

nodes.name: JiaZi
nodes.summary: The 60-term sexagenary cycle enhanced enum binding each pillar to stem, branch, number and NaYin, exposing xun-header, kongwang (Tuple2), next/previous, ten-xun listing and many ganzhi factories.

nodes.name: FiveXingGetter
nodes.summary: Extension on NaYinFiveXing adding the fiveXing getter that reads JiaZiIndexMapper.nayin_fivexing_mapper.

nodes.name: LiuQin
nodes.summary: Six-relations (六亲) enum deriving the relation from a stem/branch pair via FiveXingRelationship, or from a single Chinese character.

nodes.name: MonthGeneral
nodes.summary: Month-general (月将) enhanced enum mapping each general to its month, governing branch and the solar-term segment (Tuple2 of TwentyFourJieQi), with month/jie/branch factories.

nodes.name: EnumStars
nodes.summary: Celestial body enum (seven luminaries plus 炁罗计孛) carrying display names, five-element, yin-yang and day/night flags, with zheng/yu pairing and name lookup factories.

nodes.name: EnumStarsPriority
nodes.summary: Comparable star-priority enum (Primary..Lowest) ordering stars by a numeric weight via compareTo.

nodes.name: YuanYunOrder
nodes.summary: Three-yuan order enum (upper/middle/lower 元) with Chinese names, JSON-serializable.

nodes.name: YuanYunType
nodes.summary: Yuan/Yun period-type enum (元 = 60 years, 运 = 20 years) with name and total-years fields.

nodes.name: NineYun
nodes.summary: The nine luck periods enum, each binding an order, Chinese name and its associated NineStarEnum.

nodes.name: TianGan
nodes.summary: The ten Heavenly Stems (plus KONG_WANG) enhanced enum exposing five-element, yin-yang, na-jia trigram, five-tiger遁, clash detection, five-combine partner and ten-gods derivation.

nodes.name: TianGanFiveCombine
nodes.summary: Heavenly-stem five-combine (天干五合) enum pairing yang/yin stems (Tuple2) to a resulting element, with factories to resolve the combine by stem, element, or stem pair and combine predicates.

nodes.name: TwelveZhangSheng
nodes.summary: Twelve life-cycle stages (十二长生) enum with static mapping tables from heavenly stem and five-element to ordered branches, plus lu-branch lookup, stage-by-stem/branch derivation and strong/weak helpers.

nodes.name: TwentyFourJieQi
nodes.summary: The 24 solar terms enhanced enum carrying order, season, month-token, solar angle and ecliptic gong, with order/name factories, cyclic next/previous, jie/qi listings and eight-jie/four-li classification.

nodes.name: PillarType
nodes.summary: Chart pillar/column type enum (year/month/day/hour/ke, tai-yuan, life-house, luck cycle, liu-nian/yue/ri/shi, separators, row-title) with Chinese display names for layout rendering.

nodes.name: RowType
nodes.summary: Chart row type enum enumerating the many row kinds (stems, branches, ten-gods, na-yin, kong-wang, hidden-stems variants, star-yun, separators) used in template layouts.

nodes.name: NineStarEnum
nodes.summary: The nine stars (九星) enhanced enum, each binding number, post-heaven trigram, color, dunjia/god/ephemeris/xuankong names and five-element, with lookup factories by number, element, color, dunjia and xuankong names.

nodes.name: enum_chinese_12_zodic.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the Chinese 12-zodiac enum, mapping each animal sign to its earthly branch (DiZhi) with lookup helpers by name and branch.

nodes.name: EnumChinese12Zodiac
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum of the 12 Chinese zodiac animals, each carrying a Chinese name and associated earthly branch (DiZhi), with fromName and fromDiZhi lookups.

nodes.name: enum_datetime_type.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the DateTimeType enum distinguishing solar, lunar, and ganZhi calendar representations.

nodes.name: DateTimeType
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum representing calendar time types (solar, lunar, ganZhi) with a getFromIndex helper for ordinal lookup.

nodes.name: enum_day_night.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the EnumDayNight enum distinguishing day (昼) from night (夜) with convenience predicates.

nodes.name: EnumDayNight
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum representing day vs night periods with a Chinese name field and isDay/isNight getters.

nodes.name: enum_device_type.dart
nodes.summary: Defines a plain EnumDeviceType enum classifying client devices as mobile, tablet, desktop, or unknown.

nodes.name: EnumDeviceType
nodes.summary: Plain enum of device categories (mobile, tablet, desktop, unknown) with no associated values or methods.

nodes.name: enum_divinate_type.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the EnumDivinateType enum enumerating divination-casting methods (timing, random, number, manual, characters, sentence).

nodes.name: EnumDivinateType
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum of divination casting methods, each carrying a Chinese display name describing how a hexagram/chart is generated.

nodes.name: enum_five_xing.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the FiveXing (Five Elements) enum with the destructive-cycle (beiKe) relationship between metal, wood, water, fire, and earth.

nodes.name: FiveXing
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum of the Five Elements (金木水火土), each with a Chinese value and a beiKe getter returning the element that controls it.

nodes.name: enum_four_seasons.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the FourSeasons enum (spring/summer/autumn/winter plus earth) with mapping from a month's earthly branch (DiZhi) to its season.

nodes.name: FourSeasons
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum of four seasons plus an earth phase, with ordered indices, Chinese names, and getFourSeason mapping from a month DiZhi.

nodes.name: enum_gender.dart
nodes.summary: Defines a plain Gender enum (male, female, unknown) with JSON serialization annotations.

nodes.name: Gender
nodes.summary: Plain enum of gender values (male, female, unknown) annotated for JSON serialization.

nodes.name: enum_ji_xiong.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the JiXiongEnum (auspiciousness scale) ranging from great-fortune to great-misfortune with isJi/isXiong classifiers.

nodes.name: JiXiongEnum
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum of auspiciousness levels (大吉 through 大凶 plus unknown) with isJi/isXiong predicates and a fromName lookup defaulting to unknown.

nodes.name: enum_month_token.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the MonthToken enum binding each month's earthly branch (DiZhi) to its major-qi heavenly stem (TianGan) and five-element strength evaluation.

nodes.name: MonthToken
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum mapping each month to its DiZhi and major-qi TianGan, with fromDiZhi lookup and checkFiveXingWangShuai delegating to FiveEnergyStatus for elemental strength.

nodes.name: enum_panel_type.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the EnumPanelType enum describing chart/panel modes (single, marriage, collaboration, coopredict, verify).

nodes.name: EnumPanelType
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum of chart panel types, each carrying a Chinese display name for single, marriage, collaboration, co-prediction, and verification modes.

nodes.name: enum_query_type.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the EnumQueryType enum distinguishing destiny (命运) readings from divination (占测) queries.

nodes.name: EnumQueryType
nodes.summary: Plain enum distinguishing destiny/fortune queries from event divination queries, annotated for JSON serialization.

nodes.name: enum_ten_gods.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the EnumTenGods enum representing the Ten Gods of Bazi astrology, each with a full and single-character name.

nodes.name: EnumTenGods
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum of the Ten Gods (十神) of Bazi, each carrying a full Chinese name and a single-character short name exposed via shortName.

nodes.name: enum_twelve_ecliptic_gong.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the TwelveEclipticGong enum (zodiac constellations) with Latin names, unicode symbols, solar-angle ranges, and cross-references to TwelveStarSeq and DiZhi.

nodes.name: TwelveEclipticGong
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum of the twelve ecliptic constellations with order, Chinese/Latin names, unicode glyphs, and solar-angle ranges, plus twelveStarSeq, fromDiZhi, fromName, and fromOrder lookups.

nodes.name: enum_twelve_star_seq.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the TwelveStarSeq enum (twelve celestial star sequences) with solar-angle ranges and conversion to the corresponding TwelveEclipticGong.

nodes.name: TwelveStarSeq
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum of the twelve traditional star sequences (降娄…娵訾) with order and solar-angle ranges, exposing twelveEclipticGong conversion and fromName/fromOrder lookups.

nodes.name: enum_yin_yang.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the YinYang enum with Chinese values, binary string representation, and conversion from a binary string.

nodes.name: YinYang
nodes.summary: Enhanced enum representing yin (阴) and yang (阳) with binary-string encoding, isYang/isYin predicates, and a getByBinaryStr factory.

nodes.name: chinese_date_info.dart
nodes.summary: Aggregate domain model bundling Chinese traditional calendar data (four-pillar bazi, 72 phenology, solar terms, three-yuan/nine-yun) for a given date.

nodes.name: ChineseDateInfo
nodes.summary: Equatable JsonSerializable data class composing EightChars, Phenology, JieQiInfo and three-yuan/nine-yun fields; exposes year/month/day/time GanZhi getters delegating to eightChars. Provides fromJson/toJson and props.

nodes.name: divination_datetime.dart
nodes.summary: Core divination datetime model capturing observer, timezone handling (DST/mean-solar/true-solar), lunar date and four-pillar JiaZi, with named factory constructors for each time-correction strategy.

nodes.name: EnumDatetimeType
nodes.summary: Enum of datetime correction modes (standard, removeDST, meanSolar, trueSolar) with Chinese JsonValue labels.

nodes.name: DivinationDatetimeModel
nodes.summary: Equatable JsonSerializable model holding uuid, observer, datetime, lunar fields and four JiaZi pillars; derives bazi/lunarMonthStr/lunarDayStr getters and offers standard/removeDST/meanSolar/trueSolar factory constructors plus clone, fromJson and toJson.

nodes.name: eight_chars.dart
nodes.summary: Four-pillar (BaZi) model exposing TianGan/DiZhi decomposition and Ten Gods (shi shen) calculations against a day master.

nodes.name: EightChars
nodes.summary: Equatable JsonSerializable four-pillar class (year/month/day/time JiaZi) with TianGan/DiZhi getters, list accessors, a default-bazi factory, string serializers, and Ten Gods getters/calculators delegating to TianGan.getTenGods/DiZhi.getTenGods. Provides fromJson/toJson and props.

nodes.name: jie_qi_info.dart
nodes.summary: Solar-term (JieQi) interval model holding the current term and its start/end timestamps with previous/next term getters.

nodes.name: JieQiInfo
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable data class wrapping a TwentyFourJieQi term with startAt/endAt DateTimes and prevJieQi/nextJieQi getters. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: seventy_two_phenology.dart
nodes.summary: 72-phenology (qihou) model plus a static lookup list defining all 72 traditional phenological signs mapped to their solar terms.

nodes.name: Phenology
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable phenology data class (name, description, jieqi, order) carrying a static phenologyList of all 72 phenological entries keyed to the 24 solar terms. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: shen_sha.dart
nodes.summary: Base ShenSha (auspicious/inauspicious spirit) abstraction defining the ShenShaInterface contract plus the concrete ShenSha and lightweight ShenShaItem classes; root of the shen_sha model family.

nodes.name: ShenShaInterface
nodes.summary: Abstract interface declaring name, jiXiong (auspicious/inauspicious), descriptionList and locationDescriptionList contracts for all ShenSha types.

nodes.name: ShenSha
nodes.summary: Concrete JsonSerializable ShenSha implementing ShenShaInterface with name, jiXiong and from-JSON-only description lists; base class for the di-zhi/gan-zhi/tian-gan/zhang-sheng/bundled variants. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: ShenShaItem
nodes.summary: Lightweight ShenSha subclass carrying only name and jiXiong, with a fromShenSha factory to strip a full ShenSha down to its identity fields. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: shen_sha_bundled.dart
nodes.summary: Aggregator member of the shen_sha family defining bundled ShenSha variants (BundledShenSha with positional type/offset and OtherShenSha) used to group spirits like the twelve-palace and horse-front series.

nodes.name: BundledShenShaType
nodes.summary: Enum of bundled spirit groupings (beforeJia, afterJia, beforeHorse) with Chinese JsonValue labels.

nodes.name: BundledShenSha
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable ShenSha subclass adding a BundledShenShaType and offset (relative to HongLuan) for grouped spirit series. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: OtherShenSha
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable ShenSha subclass for miscellaneous spirits carrying name, jiXiong and description lists. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: shen_sha_di_zhi.dart
nodes.summary: Earthly-branch (DiZhi) variant of the shen_sha family; a ShenSha mapping branch positions to branches.

nodes.name: DiZhiShenSha
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable ShenSha subclass with a Map<DiZhi, DiZhi> locationMapper resolving spirit position from an earthly branch. Overrides toJson; provides fromJson.

nodes.name: shen_sha_gan_zhi.dart
nodes.summary: Stem-branch (GanZhi) variant of the shen_sha family; a ShenSha mapping earthly branches to JiaZi lists.

nodes.name: GanZhiShenSha
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable ShenSha subclass with a Map<DiZhi, List<JiaZi>> locationMapper resolving spirit positions from an earthly branch. Overrides toJson; provides fromJson.

nodes.name: shen_sha_tian_gan.dart
nodes.summary: Heavenly-stem (TianGan) variant of the shen_sha family; a ShenSha mapping stems to branches.

nodes.name: TianGanShenSha
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable ShenSha subclass with a Map<TianGan, DiZhi> locationMapper resolving spirit position from a heavenly stem. Overrides toJson; provides fromJson.

nodes.name: twelve_zhang_sheng.dart
nodes.summary: Twelve life-stages (ZhangSheng) variant of the shen_sha family; a minimal ShenSha subclass with no extra location mapping.

nodes.name: ZhangSheng12ShenSha
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable ShenSha subclass representing the twelve life-stage spirits, delegating all fields to the ShenSha super constructor. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: year_month.dart
nodes.summary: Year/month/day value object with arithmetic operators, day/hour conversions, and factory helpers for fortune-cycle (DaYun) span calculations.

nodes.name: YearMonth
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable year/month/(optional day) value object overloading +/-/comparison operators and == / hashCode, with total-month/day/hour conversions, fromTotalMonths/fromTotalDays/fromDoubleYear factories, and standard-division predicates. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: yun_yun_info.dart
nodes.summary: Three-yuan nine-yun (san-yuan jiu-yun) feng-shui period models with a generic base info class and a fromYear factory computing the cycle from a 1864 epoch.

nodes.name: EachYuanYunInfo
nodes.summary: Generic JsonSerializable (genericArgumentFactories) base class holding a YuanYunType plus start/end years and version; parent of EachYuanInfo and EachYunInfo. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: EachYuanInfo
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable subclass of EachYuanYunInfo adding a YuanYunOrder name for the three-yuan (upper/middle/lower) tier. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: EachYunInfo
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable subclass of EachYuanYunInfo adding a NineYun name for the nine-yun tier. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: ThreeYuanNineYunInfo
nodes.summary: JsonSerializable aggregate of yuanInfo/yunInfo with cycle start/end years; its fromYear factory computes the three-yuan nine-yun assignment from a 1864 epoch using 20-year yun, 60-year yuan and 180-year cycle math. Provides fromJson/toJson.

nodes.name: ConstantNineGongDataClass.dart
nodes.summary: Defines the ConstantNineGongDataClass immutable data holder representing a Nine Palace (九宫) cell with name, number, an earthly-branch tuple, and an optional heaven-gate/earth-door label.
nodes.languageNotes: Non-standard PascalCase Dart file name; uses package:tuple Tuple2 for the diZhi pair.

nodes.name: ConstantNineGongDataClass
nodes.summary: Immutable holder for a Nine Palace cell: name, number, a Tuple2 of earthly branches, and an optional heaven-gate/earth-door label.

nodes.name: calculation_strategy_config_logic_model.dart
nodes.summary: Defines CalculationStrategyConfigLogicModel, the pure business-logic configuration bundling zi-shi, solar-term, phenology, and entry-precision strategies with a default config and copyWith.
nodes.languageNotes: Equatable value type; exposes a static const defaultConfig and copyWith. Pure Dart, no Flutter dependency.

nodes.name: CalculationStrategyConfigLogicModel
nodes.summary: Equatable business-logic config holding five calendar/strategy enums; provides a static const defaultConfig and copyWith for immutable updates.

nodes.name: card_template_setting.dart
nodes.summary: JSON-serializable card template settings: CardTemplateSetting plus per-skill CardTemplateSettingOverride, with custom converters for RowType-keyed bool maps and skill-id-keyed override maps.

nodes.summary: Enum selecting the day-change boundary for the Zi hour: at 23:00 or at 0:00.

nodes.name: ChildHourMode
nodes.summary: Enum selecting the early/late Zi-hour handling mode (no-distinguish, distinguish via five-mouse-dun, distinguish fixed Zi-Ping, or 0:00-based bands).

nodes.summary: Abstract Strategy interface for deciding the day-pillar anchor (DayAnchor) for a given instant, plus the DayAnchor value object. FLUTTER-DEPENDENCY: imports package:flutter/foundation.dart inside the supposedly pure-Dart domain core (the import appears unused, a Pure-Dart contamination smell).
nodes.languageNotes: package:flutter/foundation.dart import contaminates the pure-Dart core; nothing in this file requires Flutter.

nodes.name: DayAnchor
nodes.summary: Immutable value object carrying the effective datetime used for Lunar computation and whether the next-day pillar should be used.

nodes.name: DayPillarStrategy
nodes.summary: Abstract Strategy contract: decideDayAnchor(dt) returns the anchor used to compute the day pillar. Implemented by Day0BoundaryStrategy and Day23BoundaryStrategy.

nodes.name: hour_pillar_strategy.dart
nodes.summary: Abstract Strategy interface for deciding the hour-pillar stem-branch (JiaZi) from an instant and the effective day pillar. Implemented by the five-mouse-dun, fixed Zi-Ping, and 0:00-bands strategies.

nodes.name: HourPillarStrategy
nodes.summary: Abstract Strategy contract: decideHourPillar(dt, dayPillar) returns the hour pillar JiaZi. Implemented by HourFiveMouseDunStrategy, HourFixedZiPingStrategy, and HourBandsStart0Strategy.

nodes.name: day_0_boundary_strategy.dart
nodes.summary: Concrete day-pillar Strategy using 0:00 as the day-change boundary (Zi-Zheng / traditional Zi-Ping school): the next-day pillar begins at 0:00.

nodes.name: Day0BoundaryStrategy
nodes.summary: Implements DayPillarStrategy: from 0:00 onward uses the next-day pillar; 23:00-0:00 still counts as the current day.

nodes.name: day_23_boundary_strategy.dart
nodes.summary: Concrete day-pillar Strategy using 23:00 as the day-change boundary (Zi-Chu / new Zi-Ping school): from 23:00 the instant rolls into the next day for pillar computation.

nodes.name: Day23BoundaryStrategy
nodes.summary: Implements DayPillarStrategy: at hour >= 23 advances the effective datetime by one hour into the next day and flags useNextDayPillar.

nodes.name: hour_bands_start0_strategy.dart
nodes.summary: Concrete hour-pillar Strategy dividing the full day into twelve 2-hour bands starting at 0:00 (Zi = 0:00-1:59), assigning stems via the five-mouse-dun mapping from the effective day stem.

nodes.name: HourBandsStart0Strategy
nodes.summary: Implements HourPillarStrategy: maps the hour to one of 12 bands (hour~/2) and selects the JiaZi from a 60-cycle slice starting at the five-mouse-dun stem.

nodes.name: hour_five_mouse_dun_strategy.dart
nodes.summary: Concrete hour-pillar Strategy implementing the Five-Mouse-Dun (五鼠遁) rule: derives the hour stem from the effective day stem and advances through the 60-cycle by the time-branch index.

nodes.name: HourFiveMouseDunStrategy
nodes.summary: Implements HourPillarStrategy: uses a TianGan->JiaZi start map (五鼠遁) plus ((hour+1)~/2)%12 branch index to pick the hour pillar from the 60-cycle.

nodes.name: hour_fixed_zi_ping_strategy.dart
nodes.summary: Concrete hour-pillar Strategy for the traditional Zi-Ping school: 23:00-00:00 fixed to 'N-Zi', 00:00-01:00 fixed to '(N+1)-Chou', with stems derived from the day stem; includes a conservative fallback for non-Zi hours.

nodes.name: HourFixedZiPingStrategy
nodes.summary: Implements HourPillarStrategy: composes a JiaZi from stem+branch with helpers _take12, _compose, and _hourIndexToZhi, fixing Zi/Chou branches at the 23/0 boundary.

nodes.name: layer_model_import_boundary_test.dart
nodes.summary: Layer import-boundary guard test (TASK_ID FOURZHU-META-LAYER-BOUNDARY-TEST): scans lib/src for ui/logic/repository dirs and fails if any layer file's source text references another layer's path or model file. Enforces: UI must not reference /logic/, /repository/, _logic_model.dart, or _data_model.dart; logic must not reference /ui/, /repository/, _ui_model.dart, or _data_model.dart; repository must not reference /ui/, /logic/, _ui_model.dart, or _logic_model.dart.
nodes.languageNotes: Filesystem-based string-scan guard (dart:io listSync + readAsStringSync) rather than static analysis; enforces strict three-way layer isolation across the lib/src module tree.

layers.name: Public API & Compatibility Surface
layers.description: Package barrel exports (metaphysics_core.dart, models.dart, enums.dart) plus the thin top-level *_logic/*_repository/*_ui shim files that re-export lib/src feature subsystems for backward compatibility.

layers.name: Domain Enums
layers.description: The Chinese-metaphysics domain vocabulary (tian gan, di zhi, jia zi, five xing, jie qi, stars, etc.) — a heavily-imported foundation layer (enums.dart alone has fan-in 30) with near-zero outbound dependencies.

layers.name: Domain & Data Models
layers.description: json_serializable domain models (lib/models) and DTO/persistence datamodels (lib/datamodel) — the structured data carriers (eight chars, shen sha, divination datetime, location, pillars) that depend on enums and are consumed by calculators; location.dart and chinese_date_info.dart are high fan-in hubs.

layers.name: Calculation Engine & Domain Services
layers.description: The business-logic core: da yun / four zhu / liu yun / tai yuan calculators and strategies (lib/domain), the datetime_details processor pipeline (dst/solar/timezone), the solar-lunar helper god-object, the lunar calendar adapter, the repository port interface, and application use-cases.

layers.name: Layered Feature Subsystem (lib/src)
layers.description: The newer per-feature layered architecture under lib/src — da_yun, datetime, four_zhu, liu_yun, tai_yuan each split into logic/repository/ui models with an enforced import-boundary contract (ui ↮ logic ↮ repository isolation).

layers.name: Shared Utilities
layers.description: Cross-cutting helper functions — astronomical/Julian-day and celestial rise-set math, collection/reorder helpers, constant-value lookups, and the flutter-tainted five-elements color mapping.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Test suite, including the layer-model import-boundary test that guards ui/logic/repository isolation within lib/src.

layers.name: Project Config & Documentation
layers.description: Package manifest (pubspec.yaml) configuring the whole Dart/Flutter package, plus the datetime-pipeline mermaid diagram and the test-step notes documenting module behavior.

tour.title: What This Package Exports
tour.description: Start at lib/metaphysics_core.dart, the package's public API barrel and the single import most consumers reach for. It re-exports the domain enums, the models barrel, the solar-time calculator utility, and the four_zhu_text store, so this one file is the contract that tells you everything metaphysics_core offers downstream projects. Since there is no README, this barrel is your map of the package's surface area.
tour.languageLesson: Dart libraries use 'export' directives to build barrel files: a single file re-exports many others so consumers write one import instead of dozens, while the package keeps freedom to reorganize internals.

tour.title: The Domain Vocabulary: Enums
tour.description: lib/enums.dart is the bedrock of the whole package — it has the highest fan-in (30) and fan-out (30) in the codebase, re-exporting 30 enum files. These enums (Heavenly Stems, Earthly Branches, Five Elements, Jia Zi cycle) are the shared vocabulary that every model and calculator depends on, so understanding them first makes the rest of the tour legible. Everything built later is expressed in these terms.

tour.title: Core Enum Triad: Stems, Branches, Elements
tour.description: These four files are the most heavily-imported enums in the package and form a tightly-coupled cluster (11 mutual edges). TianGan (Heavenly Stems) and DiZhi (Earthly Branches) carry rich five-element, yin-yang, and hidden-stem logic; FiveXing defines the destructive cycle between elements; and JiaZi assembles the 60-term sexagenary cycle from stems and branches. Building on the enums barrel from Step 2, this is the conceptual heart of Chinese metaphysics that the calculators operate on.
tour.languageLesson: Dart enhanced enums (enum with fields, constructors, and methods) let these domain concepts carry data and behavior — DiZhi is not just a label but knows its element, yin-yang polarity, and combination rules.

tour.title: The Data Carriers: Models Barrel
tour.description: lib/models.dart re-exports the 16 domain models that wrap the enums into structured, usable data. These are the json_serializable carriers (BaZi pillars, the shen_sha spirit family, da yun pillars, calendar info) that calculators read and produce. This step moves you from raw vocabulary (enums) to the sentences built from it (models).

tour.title: Key Domain Models: BaZi, Location, Calendar
tour.description: Three high-fan-in models anchor the data layer. EightChars (BaZi) exposes the four-pillar decomposition and Ten Gods calculations against a day master; ChineseDateInfo aggregates the full traditional-calendar bundle (pillars, phenology, solar terms) for a date; and Location (fan-in 14) is the json_serializable DTO threaded through every time-correction path. These are the structures the calculation engine consumes.
tour.languageLesson: json_serializable generates fromJson/toJson via build_runner — the @JsonSerializable annotation plus a part directive means the serialization boilerplate lives in a generated .g.dart file rather than being hand-written.

tour.title: The DateTime Hub
tour.description: lib/helpers/solar_lunar_datetime_helper.dart is a 1355-line central hub (fan-in 7, fan-out 13) that converts between solar and lunar time and computes Chinese date data — effectively the gravitational center the calculators orbit. Understanding it as the shared conversion authority explains why so many subsystems depend on it. It is acknowledged as a god-object, which is itself worth noting as a design tension in the package.

tour.title: The DateTime Processor Pipeline
tour.description: The datetime_details feature breaks time correction into a clean pipeline: the bundle calculation orchestrator composes three processors — timezone (wall-clock vs UTC), DST (daylight-saving detection and offset), and solar time (mean and true solar). This is a deliberate decomposition of the messy time logic into single-responsibility stages, contrasting with the monolithic helper from Step 6. The mermaid diagram in this folder documents the exact call flow.

tour.title: Pipeline Documentation: Mermaid Diagram
tour.description: lib/features/datetime_details/mermaid.md is a Mermaid sequence diagram capturing the datetime-details flow you just read in Step 7: the calculator delegating to the timezone, DST, and solar-time processors, each calling the Chinese-date helper. Reading the diagram alongside the code confirms your mental model of how an input instant becomes a corrected, calendar-aware datetime.
tour.languageLesson: Mermaid sequence diagrams use 'participant' and arrow syntax (->> for calls, -->> for returns) to render call flows as diagrams directly in Markdown, keeping architecture docs versioned next to the code they describe.

tour.title: The Four Pillars Engine & Strategy Pattern
tour.description: lib/domain/calculators/four_zhu/four_zhu_engine.dart computes the eight characters by composing pluggable day-pillar and hour-pillar strategies. The day_pillar_strategy interface and its sibling implementations let the package swap competing scholarly conventions (e.g. midnight vs 23:00 day boundaries) without touching the engine. This is the package's clearest example of the Strategy pattern and the core of its calculation logic.
tour.languageLesson: The Strategy pattern in Dart uses an abstract class as the interface and concrete subclasses as interchangeable algorithms — the engine holds a reference to the abstract type and stays agnostic to which implementation is plugged in.

tour.title: Fortune-Cycle Calculators & Use Cases
tour.description: Beyond the pillars engine, the domain layer computes life fortune cycles: the Tai Yuan calculator factory maps each strategy to a concrete calculator, and the yun_liu_service composes Da-Yun decades and flowing-luck periods. The usecases layer (get_da_yun_use_case) wraps these calculators behind an application-facing API that takes birth info and returns results. This shows the package's intended layering: calculators do math, use cases orchestrate them for callers.

tour.title: The Layered Subsystem (lib/src)
tour.description: The newer architecture under lib/src splits each feature into three isolated models: a logic model (pure rules, e.g. four_zhu_logic_model holding the eight pillar indices), a repository model (schema-stable persistence), and a UI model (presentation-shaped text, colors, visibility). This separation keeps business rules, storage, and rendering from leaking into each other — a cleaner contract than the god-helper of Step 6. It represents the direction the package is migrating toward.

tour.title: Enforcing the Boundary: The Guard Test
tour.description: test/layer_model_import_boundary_test.dart turns the Step 11 layering convention into an enforced contract: it scans lib/src and fails the build if a UI model imports logic, or logic imports repository, keeping the three layers isolated. This is how the package prevents architectural erosion over time — the discipline is verified, not merely documented. It is the capstone that makes the layered subsystem trustworthy.
tour.languageLesson: Dart tests can do static architecture enforcement: by reading source files from disk and asserting on their import directives, a test guards structural invariants that the type system alone cannot express.

tour.title: The Package Manifest
tour.description: pubspec.yaml declares the package's identity, Dart SDK constraints, and dependencies. Notably, despite being described as pure Dart, it pulls in Flutter transitively via google_fonts and flutter_timezone — a dependency reality worth knowing before you embed metaphysics_core in a non-Flutter context. This closes the tour by grounding everything you have seen in how the package is actually assembled and consumed.
tour.languageLesson: A Dart pubspec.yaml separates 'dependencies' (shipped at runtime) from 'dev_dependencies' (build/test only, like build_runner for json_serializable code generation); the 'environment' key pins the SDK range.
